intro:
HER is a dedicated dating app for queer women, non-binary people, and lesbians. It built one of the most trusted LGBTQ+ communities in the app space — a real achievement in an ecosystem dominated by hetero-first platforms.

Embir doesn't compete with HER's community focus. Instead, it offers something complementary: a universal platform where queer women, lesbian and bi users, and every LGBTQ+ identity are included alongside straight users — with verified profiles, deep compatibility matching, and human moderation. For users who want the safety of a verified environment without being siloed into a niche app, Embir is a strong option.

comparisonTable.competitor: Limited free, subscription for key features

sections.title: When a niche app is what you want — and when it isn't
sections.content:
HER's niche is its strength: a space explicitly built for queer women and non-binary people, where straight men can't create accounts and the community culture is shaped by and for LGBTQ+ identities.

But niche apps have a ceiling. In smaller cities or outside major urban centers, HER's user base can be thin. Bi and pan users dating across genders can find a niche app limiting. And queer couples who want to use the same platform as straight friends don't have that option with HER.

Embir includes queer women, lesbian and bi users, non-binary and trans users as full participants in a universal platform. Every orientation works the same way. You're not in a separate section — you're in the main experience.
highlights: Universal platform — no siloing of queer identities into a separate category
Works in smaller cities where niche app user bases are thin
Bi and pan users can match across orientations without switching apps

sections.title: Safety in different models
sections.content:
HER's safety model relies partly on the women-only environment — removing straight men from the equation eliminates one category of bad actor by design. This is genuinely effective at a community level.

Embir's safety model is different: systematic selfie verification, human moderation of every report, and a zero-tolerance policy regardless of gender or orientation. The founding community scale makes this feasible. Users of every gender and orientation are protected by the same infrastructure.
highlights: Human moderation team reviews every report, regardless of gender
Selfie verification applies to all users — not just women
Clear community guidelines with fast enforcement

verdict:
HER is the right choice if you want a dedicated queer women's community with strong social features and community identity.

Embir is the right choice if you want a verified, well-moderated platform where queer women and LGBTQ+ users are fully included without being separated from the broader dating community — with deep compatibility matching and free access during launch.
ctaText: Inclusive by design, verified by default — join free

faq.q: Is Embir safe for queer women and lesbian users?
faq.a: Yes. Embir has human moderation, selfie verification, and orientation-aware matching. Queer women, lesbian, bi and non-binary users are full participants in the platform — not an add-on audience.

faq.q: Can I filter matches by gender and orientation on Embir?
faq.a: Yes. Orientation and gender preferences are core to how matching works on Embir — not a secondary filter. You set your identity and preferences at signup and the algorithm respects them.
Core features — profile, discovery, messaging, compatibility — are free during the launch phase. No subscription required to start connecting.

faq.q: Does Embir have a community feature like HER?
faq.a: Embir is focused on one-to-one compatibility and connection. Community features may be added as the platform grows. The founding member community shapes the platform's direction.

faq.q: Can I use Embir if I'm bi or pan and dating across genders?
faq.a: Absolutely. Embir was built for bi, pan, and all orientation-fluid users. You're not limited to one pool — matching respects your full preferences.
